questions.question_text:
밑줄 친 부분에 들어갈 말로 가장 적절한 것을 고르시오.

The new policy is designed to _____________ unnecessary regulations that have been hindering small businesses for years.
questions.explanation: [관련 기출: 2025년 지방직 1번 / 핵심 개념: 어휘 빈칸 (제거·폐지 동사)] 정답은 ①번. 'hindering small businesses(소상공인을 방해하는)' 규제이므로 '폐지하다(abolish)'가 적절. reinforce(강화하다)는 반대 의미. preserve(보존하다)도 반대. implement(시행하다)는 새로 만드는 의미라 부적절. eradicate, abolish 같은 '제거' 동사는 2025년부터 빈칸 추론으로 단골 출제됨.

questions.question_text:
밑줄 친 부분에 들어갈 말로 가장 적절한 것을 고르시오.

Her _____________ to the deadline impressed everyone at the meeting; she never missed a single report submission throughout the year.
questions.explanation: [관련 기출: 2025년 지방직 2번(punctuality) / 핵심 개념: 어휘 빈칸 (직장 관련 명사)] 정답은 ②번. 'never missed a single report submission(보고서 제출을 한 번도 놓치지 않았다)'에서 마감 기한을 '지킴(adherence)'을 추론. indifference(무관심), hesitation(망설임), resistance(저항)는 모두 마감을 잘 지키는 태도와 반대. 2025년 지방직 punctuality 출제와 동일 계열.

questions.question_text:
밑줄 친 부분에 들어갈 말로 가장 적절한 것을 고르시오.

The research team recommended that the new vaccine _____________ tested on a larger population before being approved for public use.
questions.explanation: [관련 기출: 2023년 국가직 6번(recommended that she buy) / 핵심 개념: 제안·요구·명령 동사 + that + S + (should) + 동사원형] 정답은 ③번. 'recommend, suggest, demand, insist, propose' 등의 동사 뒤 that절에서는 'should + 동사원형'을 쓰며, should는 생략 가능하여 동사원형만 남는다. 여기서는 수동태이므로 'be tested'. is tested는 직설법이라 부적절. was tested는 시제 일치 오류. being은 분사.

questions.question_text:
밑줄 친 부분에 들어갈 말로 가장 적절한 것을 고르시오.

[메신저 대화]
Minho: Hi, I just got an email about the team workshop next Friday.
Sujin: Yes, I saw it too. Are you planning to attend?
Minho: Of course. _____________?
Sujin: It's at the conference room on the 5th floor, from 2 to 5 pm.
Minho: Got it. Should I bring anything?
Sujin: Just your laptop and a notebook.
Minho: Thanks for letting me know.

choices.text: How long will the session last
Where and when is it being held

questions.explanation: [관련 기출: 2026년 국가직 4번, 2025년 지방직 5번 / 핵심 개념: 메신저 대화 빈칸 - 직후 응답이 정답의 단서] 정답은 ④번. 빈칸 직후 'It's at the conference room on the 5th floor, from 2 to 5 pm(5층 회의실에서 2시부터 5시까지)'이 장소와 시간을 모두 답하므로 'Where and when(어디서 그리고 언제)'이 정답. 진행자, 시간 길이, 연기 이유는 응답과 일치하지 않음. 2025년부터 메신저 UI 화면이 100% 출제됨.

questions.question_text:
밑줄 친 부분에 들어갈 말로 가장 적절한 것을 고르시오.

A: Did you hear about the new restaurant that opened downtown?
B: Yes, I heard it serves authentic Italian cuisine.
A: I'd love to try it. Want to go this weekend?
B: I wish I could, but _____________.
A: Oh, that's too bad. How about next weekend then?
B: Next weekend works for me. Let's make a reservation.

choices.text: I have a family event on Saturday
I don't like Italian food at all
I've already eaten there twice this month

questions.explanation: [관련 기출: 2025년 지방직 4번(I'm on a tight budget) / 핵심 개념: 'I wish I could, but ~' 거절 표현 후 대안 제시] 정답은 ①번. 'I wish I could, but(그러고 싶지만)'은 정중한 거절. 직후 A가 '다음 주말은 어때?'라며 대안을 제시하고 B가 동의하므로, 이번 주말에 못 가는 일정상 이유가 와야 함. 이미 두 번 갔다면 다음 주말도 안 갈 것. 식당이 폐업이면 다음 주말도 의미 없음. 이탈리안을 싫어한다면 예약 자체가 모순.

questions.question_text:
[6~7] 다음 글을 읽고 물음에 답하시오. (공통 지문)

[웹페이지: https://greentownproject.org]

          (A)

The Green Town Project invites all residents to join our community-wide effort to reduce carbon emissions and create a more sustainable neighborhood.

Why participate?
Climate change is no longer a distant threat. Our daily choices matter, and collective action makes a real difference.

What we offer:
- Free monthly workshops on energy-saving techniques
- Community gardening spaces available for rent
- Subsidized electric vehicle charging stations
- Educational programs for children and teenagers

Who can join?
All residents of Green Town are welcome. No prior experience or special skills are required. Simply visit our office at 123 Main Street or register online.

When does it start?
Registration opens on March 1st, and activities begin in April. Some workshops have limited spots, so early registration is recommended.

(A)에 들어갈 윗글의 제목으로 가장 적절한 것은?

choices.text: Join the Green Town Sustainability Initiative
How to Save Money on Energy Bills

questions.explanation: [관련 기출: 2025년 국가직 6번(Join the Plastic-Free Challenge), 2025년 지방직 6번 / 핵심 개념: 공통 지문 제목 - 'Join' 동사 캠페인 참여 호소문] 정답은 ②번. 전체 글이 'invites all residents to join(모든 주민의 참여를 권유)'로 시작하는 캠페인 안내문이며, 지속가능성(sustainable)을 강조하므로 'Sustainability Initiative'가 적절. 재활용 규정은 글에 없음. 에너지 절약은 일부 내용일 뿐. 전기차는 일부 혜택일 뿐. 2025년부터 'Join the ~ Challenge/Initiative' 형식 제목이 정형화.

questions.question_text: 윗글의 내용과 일치하는 것은? (6번 공통지문 문제)
questions.explanation: [관련 기출: 2025년 국가직 7번, 2025년 지방직 7번 / 핵심 개념: 공통 지문 내용 일치 - 세부 정보 대조] 정답은 ③번. 'Some workshops have limited spots(일부 워크숍은 정원이 제한적)'와 정확히 일치. 'No prior experience or special skills are required(사전 경험이나 특별한 기술이 필요 없다)'와 반대. 'Free monthly workshops(무료 월간 워크숍)'와 반대. 'Registration opens on March 1st, and activities begin in April(등록은 3월 1일, 활동은 4월)'이므로 등록 개시일이 다름.

questions.question_text:
[8~9] 다음 글을 읽고 물음에 답하시오. (공통 지문)

[웹페이지: National Library Services]

Library Card Application

Dear valued community member,

Thank you for your interest in obtaining a library card with the National Library Services. We are pleased to assist you in accessing our extensive collection of books, digital resources, and educational programs.

To complete your application, please submit the following documents:
- A valid photo identification (driver's license, passport, or national ID card)
- Proof of current residential address (utility bill or rental agreement dated within the past three months)
- A completed application form, which can be downloaded from our website or picked up at any branch

Processing typically takes 3-5 business days. Once your application is approved, we will dispatch your library card to your registered address. Should you have any questions about your application status, please contact our customer service desk during regular business hours.

We are committed to serving our community and look forward to welcoming you as a member of our library family.

밑줄 친 dispatch의 의미와 가장 가까운 것은?
questions.explanation: [관련 기출: 2025년 국가직 8번(assess=evaluate), 2025년 지방직 8번(critical=pivotal) / 핵심 개념: 공통 지문 문맥 어휘 동의어] 정답은 ④번. dispatch는 '발송하다, 보내다'의 의미로 send와 동의어. 'we will dispatch your library card to your registered address(등록된 주소로 도서관 카드를 발송할 것)'의 문맥에서 명확. discard(버리다), duplicate(복제하다), destroy(파괴하다)는 모두 카드 발송 문맥에 부적절. 공통 지문 어휘 문제는 항상 문맥 단서로 추론.

questions.question_text: 윗글의 목적으로 가장 적절한 것은? (8번 공통지문 문제)

choices.text: to explain how to apply for a library card
to request donations for library services
to announce a new library opening

questions.explanation: [관련 기출: 2025년 국가직 9번(to explain how to file complaints) / 핵심 개념: 공통 지문 글의 목적] 정답은 ①번. 제목이 'Library Card Application(도서관 카드 신청)'이며, 본문 전체가 신청 시 제출 서류와 절차를 안내하므로 '카드 신청 방법 설명'이 목적. 새 도서관 개관 안내가 아님. 프로그램 광고가 아닌 절차 안내. 기부 요청 내용은 전혀 없음.

questions.question_text:
다음 글의 목적으로 가장 적절한 것은?

[이메일]
To: contact@cityhall.gov
From: jchoi@email.com
Date: May 15, 2026
Subject: Request for Streetlight Repair

Dear City Maintenance Department,

I am writing to bring to your attention an urgent issue in our neighborhood. The streetlights along Oak Avenue, between 5th Street and 8th Street, have been malfunctioning for over two weeks. Several lights flicker intermittently, while others remain completely dark throughout the night.

This situation has raised serious safety concerns among residents. The area has become particularly dangerous for pedestrians, especially elderly residents and children returning from school activities. Additionally, there have been reports of minor traffic incidents at the intersection due to poor visibility.

I would greatly appreciate it if your department could arrange for an inspection and repair of these streetlights as soon as possible. Many residents in our community have expressed similar concerns, and prompt action would significantly improve safety in our neighborhood.

Thank you for your attention to this matter. I look forward to your response.

Sincerely,
Jiyoung Choi
Resident, Oak Avenue

choices.text: to express gratitude for recent repairs
to propose new street lighting installation

questions.explanation: [관련 기출: 2025년 국가직 11번(to solicit the Council), 2026년 국가직 10번 / 핵심 개념: 이메일 글의 목적] 정답은 ②번. 'I would greatly appreciate it if your department could arrange for an inspection and repair of these streetlights(점검과 수리를 해주시면 감사하겠다)'에서 명확히 수리 요청 목적. 감사가 아닌 요청. 교통 위반 항의가 아닌 안전 문제 제기. 신규 설치가 아닌 기존 가로등 수리.

questions.question_text:
다음 글의 내용과 일치하지 않는 것은?

[캠프 안내]
SUMMER CODING ACADEMY 2026

Join our exciting two-week coding camp for young learners!

WHO
For students ages 10-16
No prior coding experience necessary

WHAT
Participants will learn Python programming, web development basics, and create their own mobile app prototype. Our experienced instructors provide personalized guidance based on each student's skill level.

WHEN
Session 1: July 6 - July 17 (Mon-Fri, 9 am - 3 pm)
Session 2: July 20 - July 31 (Mon-Fri, 9 am - 3 pm)

WHERE
Digital Innovation Center, 456 Technology Park

FEES
$300 per session (includes lunch, materials, and certification)
Scholarship available for students from low-income families

FINAL SHOWCASE
All participants will present their projects at the closing ceremony, where families and friends are welcomed to attend.

choices.text: 코딩 경험이 없는 학생도 참가할 수 있다.

questions.explanation: [관련 기출: 2025년 국가직 14번(KIDS SUMMER ART CAMP) / 핵심 개념: 안내문 내용 일치 - 세부 정보 대조] 정답은 ③번. 'Mon-Fri(월~금)'로 명시되어 있어 주말은 운영하지 않음. 'No prior coding experience necessary'와 일치. 'includes lunch, materials, and certification'과 일치. 'Scholarship available for students from low-income families'와 일치. 안내문 일치 문제는 시간·요일·비용 같은 구체 정보의 함정에 주의.

questions.question_text:
다음 글에서 The Coastal Marine Research Institute에 대한 내용과 일치하는 것은?

[웹페이지: cmri.org]

The Coastal Marine Research Institute

The Coastal Marine Research Institute (CMRI) is dedicated to the conservation and study of marine ecosystems along our nation's coastlines. Founded in 1985, the Institute has grown into one of the leading marine research centers in the region.

Our Mission
- Scientific Research:
We conduct year-round investigations on marine biodiversity, ocean pollution, and the impacts of climate change on coastal environments.

- Community Education:
Through public lectures, school visits, and our visitor center exhibitions, we engage citizens of all ages in understanding marine conservation.

- Policy Advocacy:
CMRI works closely with government agencies to develop evidence-based policies for sustainable coastal management.

- International Cooperation:
We collaborate with marine research institutions worldwide, sharing data and expertise to address global ocean challenges.

Visit our headquarters in Coastville to learn more about ongoing projects and how you can support our work.

choices.text: It was established in the early 21st century.
Its research is limited to summer months.
It focuses solely on domestic projects.
It engages people of all age groups in marine conservation.

questions.explanation: [관련 기출: 2025년 국가직 15번(HHS), 2025년 지방직 11번 / 핵심 개념: 기관 소개 내용 일치] 정답은 ④번. 'we engage citizens of all ages in understanding marine conservation(모든 연령대 시민이 해양 보존을 이해하도록)'과 일치. 'Founded in 1985(1985년 설립)'이므로 20세기 후반. 'year-round investigations(연중 조사)'와 반대. 'collaborate with marine research institutions worldwide(전 세계 연구기관과 협력)'이므로 국내에 국한되지 않음.

questions.question_text:
밑줄 친 부분 중 어법상 옳지 않은 것을 고르시오.

The ancient library of Alexandria, ①which was once considered the greatest center of learning in the ancient world, ②housed an estimated 400,000 to 700,000 scrolls at its peak. Scholars from across the Mediterranean traveled there to study and ③copying valuable texts. Unfortunately, a series of fires over several centuries ④destroyed much of this irreplaceable collection.
questions.explanation: [관련 기출: 2025년 국가직 12·13번, 2025년 지방직 13·14번 / 핵심 개념: 어법 밑줄 - 병렬 구조] 정답은 ①번. 'to study and copying'은 병렬 구조 오류. 'to study and (to) copy'로 동사원형(원형부정사)이 와야 함. which는 계속적 용법 관계대명사로 적절. housed는 동사로 적절(주어 The ancient library). destroyed는 과거 시제 동사로 적절. 등위접속사 and/but/or 앞뒤는 항상 같은 품사·형태를 유지해야 함.

questions.question_text:
밑줄 친 부분 중 어법상 옳지 않은 것을 고르시오.

Researchers have long known ①that sleep plays a crucial role in memory consolidation. During deep sleep, the brain ②processes information acquired during the day, strengthening neural connections ③which are essential for long-term retention. Studies show that students ④what get adequate sleep before exams perform significantly better than those who stay up all night cramming.
questions.explanation: [관련 기출: 2025년 국가직 13번, 2026년 국가직 13번 / 핵심 개념: 어법 밑줄 - 관계대명사 what vs who] 정답은 ②번. 'students what get adequate sleep'에서 what은 선행사를 포함하는 관계대명사라 선행사(students)가 따로 있으면 쓸 수 없음. 사람이 선행사이고 주격이므로 who/that이 와야 함 → 'students who get adequate sleep'. that은 know의 목적어 명사절 접속사로 적절. processes는 단수 주어 the brain과 수일치. which는 사물 선행사(neural connections)를 받는 주격 관계대명사로 적절.

questions.question_text:
다음 글의 주제로 가장 적절한 것은?

Recent studies in cognitive psychology have revealed that taking handwritten notes leads to deeper learning compared to typing on a laptop. While typing allows students to record more words verbatim, this very ease becomes a disadvantage. When typing, students often transcribe lectures word-for-word without processing the meaning. Handwriting, by contrast, is slower and forces the writer to summarize and synthesize information in real time. This cognitive effort engages multiple regions of the brain associated with comprehension and memory. Furthermore, the physical act of forming letters by hand activates motor pathways that reinforce neural encoding. Studies tracking student performance over a semester found that handwriting groups consistently outperformed typing groups on conceptual questions, though the two groups performed similarly on simple factual recall tests. These findings suggest that despite the convenience of digital tools, traditional handwriting remains a more effective method for meaningful learning.

choices.text: different note-taking styles among university students
the historical evolution of note-taking methods
cognitive benefits of handwriting over typing in learning
the importance of digital literacy in modern classrooms

questions.explanation: [관련 기출: 2025년 지방직 15번, 2026년 국가직 15번 / 핵심 개념: 글의 주제 - 비교·대조 글] 정답은 ③번. 글 전체가 손글씨(handwriting)와 타이핑(typing)을 비교하며, 손글씨가 학습에 더 효과적이라는 인지적 이유들을 제시. 첫 문장 'taking handwritten notes leads to deeper learning compared to typing'이 주제문. 역사적 변천이 아닌 효과 비교. 디지털 리터러시는 부수적 언급. 학생 유형별 스타일이 아니라 방식의 효과 비교.

questions.question_text:
주어진 문장이 들어갈 위치로 가장 적절한 것은?

However, this strategy backfires when the predator is unfamiliar with the warning signal.

Many animals in nature use bright coloration as a defense mechanism. ( ① ) Species such as poison dart frogs and monarch butterflies display vivid colors that signal to potential predators that they are toxic or distasteful. ( ② ) Predators that have had negative experiences with such prey learn to avoid them, creating a clear evolutionary advantage. ( ③ ) Young or inexperienced predators may attack the colorful prey, often resulting in death for the prey despite its chemical defenses. ( ④ ) Therefore, the effectiveness of warning coloration depends heavily on the predator's prior learning experiences in the ecosystem.
questions.explanation: [관련 기출: 2025년 국가직 16번, 2025년 지방직 17번 / 핵심 개념: 문장 삽입 - 역접 연결어(However)] 정답은 ④번. 주어진 문장은 'However ~ backfires(역효과를 낸다) when the predator is unfamiliar(포식자가 신호를 모를 때)'로 앞 내용의 반대 상황을 제시. 앞까지는 경고색의 성공 사례. 다음 문장이 'Young or inexperienced predators(어리거나 경험 없는 포식자)'가 공격하는 실패 사례를 구체화하므로 자리가 정확. ④자리에 넣으면 바로 다음의 결론(Therefore)과 어색하게 연결됨.

questions.question_text:
다음 글의 흐름상 어색한 문장은?

Urban farming has emerged as a promising solution to several modern challenges. ①Rooftop gardens, vertical farms, and community plots in city centers provide fresh produce while reducing the carbon footprint associated with long-distance food transportation. ②These spaces also serve as green refuges in densely populated areas, improving air quality and providing habitats for pollinators like bees and butterflies. ③Traditional rural farming methods have remained largely unchanged for generations, with farmers relying on inherited techniques passed down through families. ④Furthermore, urban farming initiatives often involve local residents, fostering a sense of community and educating young people about sustainable food production. As cities continue to grow worldwide, integrating agriculture into urban planning will become increasingly important.
questions.explanation: [관련 기출: 2025년 국가직 17번, 2024년 지방직 16번 / 핵심 개념: 흐름상 어색한 문장 - 주제 이탈] 정답은 ①번. 글의 주제는 '도시 농업(urban farming)'인데, '전통적인 시골 농업 방식(Traditional rural farming methods)'을 다루므로 주제와 무관. 도시 농업 형태와 이점, 환경적 이점, 공동체 형성 효과로 모두 주제에 부합. '어색한 문장' 유형은 핵심 키워드가 주제에서 벗어나는지 확인하는 것이 관건.

questions.question_text:
주어진 글 다음에 이어질 글의 순서로 가장 적절한 것은?

The concept of 'flow state' was first introduced by psychologist Mihaly Csikszentmihalyi in the 1970s.

(A) During flow, individuals experience a sense of timelessness, intense focus, and intrinsic satisfaction. Hours can pass without notice, and the activity becomes its own reward.

(B) He observed that artists, athletes, and creative professionals often described a state of complete absorption in their work, where they performed at their peak while feeling effortless engagement.

(C) Csikszentmihalyi identified specific conditions that promote this state: clear goals, immediate feedback, and a balance between challenge and skill level. When tasks are too easy, boredom arises; when too difficult, anxiety takes over.
questions.explanation: [관련 기출: 2025년 지방직 16번, 2026년 국가직 18번 / 핵심 개념: 글의 순서 - 도입→관찰→설명→조건 흐름] 정답은 ②번. 주어진 글: 칙센트미하이가 '몰입'을 도입(1970s). (B): 'He observed(그가 관찰했다)'로 He=칙센트미하이를 받으며 어떻게 발견했는지 설명. (A): 'During flow(몰입 중에)' 일어나는 경험을 묘사 - 시간감각 상실, 집중. (C): 'Csikszentmihalyi identified specific conditions(특정 조건을 규명)'로 몰입의 조건을 추가 설명. 따라서 (B)→(A)→(C).

questions.question_text:
밑줄 친 부분에 들어갈 말로 가장 적절한 것을 고르시오.

In an age of constant connectivity, the ability to disconnect has become a rare and valuable skill. Researchers studying the effects of digital overload have discovered that our brains, which evolved to handle face-to-face interactions and immediate physical surroundings, struggle to cope with the relentless flow of notifications, messages, and updates. The constant stream of digital input keeps our nervous systems in a state of low-grade alert, never fully relaxing or engaging with the present moment. Studies show that even brief, intentional periods of digital silence—a walk without a phone, a meal without screens, a conversation without interruptions—can significantly restore mental clarity and emotional balance. The benefit comes not from rejecting technology, but from reclaiming _____________. When we choose when and how to engage with our devices rather than reacting to every alert, we transform technology from a master back into a tool.
questions.explanation: [관련 기출: 2025년 국가직 19번(a high degree of self-control), 2025년 지방직 19번 / 핵심 개념: 긴 지문 빈칸 추론 - 문맥 핵심 개념] 정답은 ③번. 빈칸 직후 'When we choose when and how to engage with our devices rather than reacting to every alert(모든 알림에 반응하는 대신 언제 어떻게 기기를 다룰지 선택할 때)'에서 핵심은 '주의(attention)에 대한 통제(control)'. 따라서 'control over our attention'이 정답. 디지털 혁신은 글의 취지(거리두기)와 반대. 전통적 통신 방식은 언급 없음. 직장 생산성은 글의 초점이 아닌 정신 건강.

questions.question_text:
밑줄 친 부분에 들어갈 말로 가장 적절한 것을 고르시오.

The principle of diminishing returns is one of the most fundamental concepts in economics, but its lessons extend far beyond financial decisions. Initially, adding more resources to any endeavor produces dramatic improvements: the first hour of studying for an exam yields enormous gains in understanding, the first employees hired transform a startup, the first servings of food bring genuine satisfaction to a hungry person. However, _____________. The tenth hour of studying may produce little additional learning as fatigue sets in, the hundredth employee may add bureaucratic complexity rather than productivity, and the third dessert may bring more discomfort than pleasure. This pattern repeats across domains: exercise, relationships, even creative work. Recognizing where the curve flattens—where additional input no longer justifies the cost—is one of the most valuable skills in personal and professional life.

choices.text: investing more resources always guarantees better results
these initial gains tend to multiply exponentially over time
external factors usually determine the final outcome
each additional input eventually yields smaller benefits

questions.explanation: [관련 기출: 2026년 국가직 20번(For every benefit gained, there is usually some cost), 2025년 지방직 20번 / 핵심 개념: 긴 지문 빈칸 추론 - 지문 전체 요지 압축] 정답은 ④번. 'diminishing returns(수확 체감)' 원리는 '추가 투입의 효과가 점점 줄어든다'는 의미. 빈칸 다음 예시들(10번째 학습 시간, 100번째 직원, 3번째 디저트) 모두 추가 투입의 가치가 떨어지는 사례이므로 'each additional input eventually yields smaller benefits'가 정답. 'exponentially(기하급수적으로) 증가'는 정반대. 외부 요인 결정은 글의 초점이 아님. 더 많은 자원이 더 나은 결과를 보장한다는 것은 글의 주장과 정반대.
